content: Child health interventions span multiple levels: (1) Preventive care—immunization schedule (CDC/ACIP recommendations), well-child visits with developmental surveillance, nutrition counseling, injury prevention guidance; (2) Nutrition programs—breastfeeding support (Baby-Friendly Hospital Initiative), WIC program, school meal programs, SNAP benefits; (3) Early intervention—Part C IDEA services for developmental delays, early childhood education (Head Start), home visiting programs (Nurse-Family Partnership); (4) Mental health—screening for ACEs, behavioral health integration in pediatrics, school-based mental health services; (5) Policy approaches—child tax credits, paid parental leave, safe housing codes, lead abatement, gun safety laws, car seat legislation; (6) Special populations—children with medical complexity care coordination, foster care health oversight, refugee health screening. Medical home model provides comprehensive, coordinated, family-centered care. Two-generation approaches address parent and child needs simultaneously.
